overview.purpose: Define the functional and non-functional requirements for a secure To-Do List backend service for pilots.

inScope: Task creation, update, completion, and deletion
Audit logging for all task modifications
Data encryption at rest and in transit

definitions.definition: A single unit of work tracked by the application.

functional.title: Create Task
functional.description: The system shall allow an authenticated user to create a new task with a title and optional description.
acceptanceCriteria: User can enter a task title and optional description
Task is added to the user's task list

functional.title: Update Task
functional.description: The system shall allow an authenticated user to update the title and description of their own tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: User can modify the title and description of an existing task
Changes are reflected in the user's task list

functional.title: Complete Task
functional.description: The system shall allow an authenticated user to mark their own tasks as completed or not completed.
acceptanceCriteria: User can toggle the completion status of a task
Task status is updated in the user's task list

functional.title: Delete Task
functional.description: The system shall allow an authenticated user to delete their own tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: User can delete a task from their task list
Task is removed from the system

functional.title: Retrieve Tasks
functional.description: The system shall provide an API to retrieve all tasks for the authenticated user, including their completion status.
acceptanceCriteria: User can request a list of all their tasks
Each task includes its completion status

functional.title: Authentication and Access Control
functional.description: All API endpoints shall require authentication and enforce user-level access control.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can only access their own tasks

nonFunctional.description: All data at rest shall be encrypted using industry-standard algorithms (e.g., AES-256 or better).
All data in transit shall be encrypted using TLS 1.3 or higher.
The system shall log all access and modification events to tasks, including user ID, timestamp, and action.
The system shall reject requests with malformed or invalid input and return appropriate error codes.
The system shall respond to 95% of API requests within 500ms under normal load.
The system shall be resistant to common web vulnerabilities, including but not limited to SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF.

openQuestions.question: What authentication mechanism should be used (e.g., OAuth2, company SSO, etc.)?
What is the expected maximum number of concurrent users?
Are there any regulatory requirements for data retention or deletion?
Should the system support multi-device synchronization for tasks?
